title: athyrium pectinatum
intro: Athyrium pectinatum, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Athyrium and is native to the mountainous regions of China. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Athyrium pectinatum include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. Athyrium pectinatum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts have also been used in various herbal preparations to support overall health and well-being.
intro_study: According to "Journal of traditional and complementary medicine", athyrium pectinatum benefits include its use in traditional medicine to treat ailments such as cough, rheumatic pain, and breast pain during childbirth, as well as its potential as an antiparasitic and antioxidant.
